 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-c6q","title":"FEAT: Real-Time Agent Collaboration Graph","description":"## Overview

Interactive visualization showing agent coordination in real-time. The Collaboration Graph provides a dynamic, force-directed graph view of all active agents, their resource reservations, and inter-agent communication patterns within a session.

## Background & Reasoning

As multi-agent systems grow in complexity, understanding coordination dynamics becomes critical. Operators frequently ask:

- **"Why is this agent waiting?"** - Visualize blocking dependencies and resource contention
- **"Who has file X?"** - Instantly see reservation ownership across the agent pool
- **"What's the communication flow?"** - Trace message paths and handoff sequences
- **"Where are the bottlenecks?"** - Identify coordination hotspots and deadlock risks

Without visibility into agent coordination, debugging multi-agent workflows becomes guesswork. The Collaboration Graph transforms abstract coordination state into intuitive visual patterns.

## Technical Architecture

### Graph Data Model

```typescript
interface AgentNode {
  id: string;
  agentId: string;
  status: 'active' | 'idle' | 'waiting' | 'blocked';
  currentTask?: string;
  reservationCount: number;
  messagesSent: number;
  messagesReceived: number;
}

interface ReservationNode {
  id: string;
  resourcePath: string;
  resourceType: 'file' | 'directory' | 'lock';
  holderId: string;
  waiters: string[];
  acquiredAt: Date;
}

interface ConflictNode {
  id: string;
  conflictType: 'deadlock' | 'contention' | 'timeout';
  involvedAgents: string[];
  involvedResources: string[];
  severity: 'warning' | 'critical';
  detectedAt: Date;
}
```

### Edge Types

| Edge Type | Description | Visual Style |
|-----------|-------------|--------------|
| `message` | Agent-to-agent communication | Animated dashed line, directional arrow |
| `handoff` | Task delegation between agents | Thick solid line, pulse animation |
| `dependency` | Blocking relationship (waiting for resource) | Red dotted line when blocked |
| `reservation` | Agent owns resource | Green solid line to reservation node |
| `waiting` | Agent waiting for resource | Orange dashed line to reservation node |

### Real-Time Updates

```typescript
// WebSocket subscription for graph updates
const subscriptions = {
  'agent.status': updateAgentNode,
  'reservation.acquired': addReservationEdge,
  'reservation.released': removeReservationEdge,
  'message.sent': animateMessageEdge,
  'conflict.detected': highlightConflictNode,
  'conflict.resolved': removeConflictNode,
};
```

Updates are batched at 100ms intervals to prevent UI thrashing during high-activity periods.

### View Modes

| Mode | Shows | Use Case |
|------|-------|----------|
| **Agents Only** | Agent nodes + message/handoff edges | Communication flow analysis |
| **Files** | Agent nodes + reservation nodes + ownership edges | Resource contention debugging |
| **Full** | All nodes + all edges | Complete system state |

### Layout Algorithm

- **Base**: Force-directed layout using D3-force
- **Clustering**: Semantic grouping by active task similarity
- **Collision**: Node repulsion prevents overlap
- **Centering**: Active/blocked agents pulled toward center
- **Stabilization**: Layout settles within 500ms of changes

## Key Components

```
apps/web/src/components/collaboration/
├── CollaborationGraph.tsx       # Main container, manages state & subscriptions
├── nodes/
│   ├── AgentGraphNode.tsx       # Agent visualization with status indicators
│   ├── ReservationGraphNode.tsx # Resource node with holder/waiter counts
│   └── ConflictGraphNode.tsx    # Conflict alert with severity styling
├── edges/
│   ├── MessageEdge.tsx          # Animated message flow visualization
│   ├── HandoffEdge.tsx          # Task delegation indicator
│   └── DependencyEdge.tsx       # Blocking relationship with state color
├── controls/
│   ├── ViewModeSelector.tsx     # Toggle between view modes
│   ├── GraphLegend.tsx          # Visual key for node/edge types
│   └── GraphStats.tsx           # Real-time metrics panel
├── hooks/
│   ├── useGraphSubscription.ts  # WebSocket subscription management
│   ├── useGraphLayout.ts        # D3-force layout integration
│   └── useGraphSelection.ts     # Node/edge selection state
└── utils/
    ├── graphTransforms.ts       # API data to React Flow format
    └── layoutConfig.ts          # D3-force parameters
```

## Technology

- **React Flow** (`@xyflow/react`) - Graph rendering and interaction
- **D3-force** - Physics-based layout algorithm
- **WebSocket** - Real-time state synchronization
- **Zustand** - Graph state management

## File Locations

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Main graph component | `apps/web/src/components/collaboration/CollaborationGraph.tsx` |
| Graph nodes | `apps/web/src/components/collaboration/nodes/` |
| Graph edges | `apps/web/src/components/collaboration/edges/` |
| Graph hooks | `apps/web/src/components/collaboration/hooks/` |
| Graph utilities | `apps/web/src/components/collaboration/utils/` |
| Graph tests | `apps/web/src/components/collaboration/__tests__/` |

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Graph layout algorithm produces stable positions
- [ ] Node components render correct status indicators
- [ ] Edge components animate on message events
- [ ] View mode filtering shows/hides correct elements
- [ ] Graph transforms handle malformed API data

### Integration Tests
- [ ] WebSocket subscription receives and processes updates
- [ ] Multiple rapid updates batch correctly
- [ ] Disconnection/reconnection maintains graph state
- [ ] Selection state persists across updates

### E2E Tests
- [ ] View mode switching updates graph immediately
- [ ] Clicking agent node opens agent detail panel
- [ ] Clicking reservation node shows resource info
- [ ] Graph renders correctly with 10+ agents
- [ ] Conflict highlighting draws user attention

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Graph renders 50+ agents at 60fps
- [ ] Layout stabilizes within 500ms
- [ ] Memory usage stable over 1hr session
- [ ] WebSocket message processing < 10ms

### Logging
- [ ] Graph tests log node/edge counts, batch sizes, and layout stabilization timing for diagnosis
- [ ] WebSocket reconnection tests log replay counts and expired cursors (no secrets)


## Logging Requirements

### Graph State Changes
```typescript
logger.debug('graph.node.added', { nodeType, nodeId, timestamp });
logger.debug('graph.edge.added', { edgeType, source, target });
logger.debug('graph.layout.stabilized', { nodeCount, edgeCount, duration });
```

### WebSocket Metrics
```typescript
logger.info('graph.subscription.connected', { sessionId });
logger.warn('graph.subscription.reconnecting', { attempt, backoff });
logger.info('graph.messages.processed', { count, batchDuration });
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Graph displays all active agents with correct status indicators
- [ ] Reservation ownership edges update within 200ms of acquisition
- [ ] Message animations visible for inter-agent communication
- [ ] Conflict nodes appear immediately when detected
- [ ] View mode toggle switches display within 100ms
- [ ] Graph legend accurately describes all visual elements
- [ ] Stats panel shows real-time agent/reservation counts
- [ ] Graph remains responsive with 50+ concurrent agents
- [ ] Keyboard navigation supported for accessibility
- [ ] Graph state persists across browser refresh (session storage)

## Reference

PLAN.md §22.4 - Real-Time Agent Collaboration Graph

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-d18","title":"FEAT: Structured Logging + Correlation IDs + Audit Pipeline","description":"## Background

Observability is foundational for operating a production agent orchestration system. Without structured logging and correlation IDs, debugging distributed request flows becomes nearly impossible. The audit pipeline provides compliance capabilities and operational insights.

## Reasoning

### Why Structured JSON Logging?
- **Machine Parseable**: JSON logs can be ingested by log aggregation systems (ELK, Datadog, CloudWatch)
- **Consistent Schema**: All log entries follow a predictable format with timestamp, level, service, message, and metadata
- **Queryable**: Enables powerful filtering and analysis across all system components
- **Production Ready**: Essential for SRE workflows and incident response

### Why Correlation IDs?
- **Request Tracing**: Track a single user request through gateway, agent service, and all downstream components
- **Debugging**: When something fails, find all related log entries instantly
- **Performance Analysis**: Measure latency across the full request lifecycle
- **multi-workspace Safety**: Ensure logs can be filtered per-workspace for support inquiries

### Why Audit Pipeline?
- **Compliance**: Many advanced customers require audit trails for agent actions
- **Security**: Detect and investigate suspicious activity patterns
- **Analytics**: Understand system usage patterns and optimize accordingly
- **Accountability**: Track which user/API key triggered which agent operations

## Technical Considerations

### Logging Implementation
- Use `pino` for high-performance JSON logging (10x faster than winston)
- Configure log levels: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal
- Default to `info` in production, `debug` in development
- Implement log rotation and size limits for local development
- Redact sensitive fields: API keys, tokens, passwords, PII

### Correlation ID Strategy
- Generate UUID v7 (time-ordered) for new requests at gateway entry
- Accept incoming `X-Correlation-ID` header to support distributed tracing
- Propagate via AsyncLocalStorage for automatic inclusion in all logs
- Include in all HTTP responses for client-side correlation
- Pass to spawned agents via environment variable

### Audit Event Schema
```typescript
interface AuditEvent {
  id: string;                    // UUID v7
  timestamp: string;             // ISO 8601
  correlationId: string;         // Request correlation
  workspaceId: string;              // multi-workspace isolation
  userId?: string;               // Authenticated user
  apiKeyId?: string;             // API key used
  action: AuditAction;           // Enum of auditable actions
  resource: string;              // Resource identifier
  resourceType: ResourceType;    // agent, session, output, etc.
  outcome: 'success' | 'failure';
  metadata: Record<string, unknown>;
  ipAddress?: string;            // For security analysis
  userAgent?: string;            // Client identification
}
```

### Sensitive Data Redaction
- API keys: Show only last 4 characters
- Passwords: Replace entirely with `[REDACTED]`
- Email addresses: Partial redaction `j***@example.com`
- Custom redaction rules via configuration

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All HTTP requests receive correlation ID (generated or propagated)
- [ ] All log entries include: timestamp, level, correlationId, service, message
- [ ] Sensitive fields are automatically redacted in logs
- [ ] Log level is configurable via environment variable
- [ ] Audit events are emitted for: agent spawn, agent terminate, session create, authentication
- [ ] Audit events are written to both log stream and audit table
- [ ] Correlation ID appears in HTTP response headers
- [ ] AsyncLocalStorage correctly propagates context through async operations
- [ ] Unit tests for redaction logic with edge cases
- [ ] Integration test for correlation ID propagation

## File Locations

### Middleware
- `apps/gateway/src/middleware/correlation.middleware.ts` - Correlation ID generation/propagation
- `apps/gateway/src/middleware/logging.middleware.ts` - Request/response logging

### Services
- `apps/gateway/src/services/audit.service.ts` - Audit event creation and persistence
- `apps/gateway/src/services/logger.service.ts` - Configured pino logger instance

### Utilities
- `apps/gateway/src/utils/redaction.ts` - Sensitive data redaction functions
- `apps/gateway/src/utils/async-context.ts` - AsyncLocalStorage helpers

### Configuration
- `apps/gateway/src/config/logging.config.ts` - Log levels, formats, destinations

### Types
- `packages/shared-types/src/audit.types.ts` - Audit event interfaces and enums

## Reference

- PLAN.md §24: Observability and Logging
- OpenTelemetry Logging Data Model for schema inspiration
- OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet for security considerations


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Correlation ID generation/propagation across middleware, services, and WS events
- [ ] Redaction utilities remove secrets deterministically
- [ ] Audit event builder produces schema-valid events with required metadata

### Integration Tests
- [ ] REST request with correlation header → response + logs include same correlationId
- [ ] WS event emission includes correlationId and matches event schema

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Logging backend unavailable → non-fatal fallback without dropping requests

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Covered by `flywheel_gateway-tz4` critical-path E2E suite; add a focused E2E spec if this feature introduces unique user workflows
- [ ] Failure mode E2E validates actionable recovery UI/messages (with artifacts captured)

### Logging
- [ ] Tests assert logs are structured JSON and contain expected keys (no console.log noise)

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-d8b","title":"TASK: Testing Infrastructure and Standards","description":"## Overview
Establish comprehensive testing infrastructure, patterns, and standards that apply across all Flywheel Gateway features. This bead defines the testing strategy that all other beads must follow.

## Background & Reasoning
Testing is not an afterthought - it's integral to each feature. This bead establishes:
1. **Consistent test structure** across the monorepo
2. **Shared test utilities** for common patterns
3. **Performance baselines** that guard against regressions
4. **E2E test framework** for critical user journeys
5. **Detailed logging in tests** so failures are diagnosable

Every feature bead must include testing requirements that reference these standards.

## Testing Pyramid

### Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- **Framework**: Bun test (native, fast)
- **Location**: `*.test.ts` co-located with source
- **Scope**: Individual functions, classes, React components
- **Mocking**: Prefer dependency injection over mocking
- **Speed**: < 100ms per test file

### Integration Tests (70% coverage target)
- **Framework**: Bun test with test database
- **Location**: `tests/integration/`
- **Scope**: API routes with real database, service combinations
- **Database**: Fresh SQLite per test suite (in-memory or temp file)
- **Speed**: < 5s per test file

### E2E Tests (Critical paths)
- **Framework**: Playwright
- **Location**: `tests/e2e/`
- **Scope**: Full user journeys through UI
- **Browser**: Chromium, Firefox, Safari (CI matrix)
- **Artifacts**: Screenshots/videos on failure

### Contract Tests (100% API coverage)
- **Framework**: Bun test + OpenAPI validator
- **Location**: `tests/contract/`
- **Scope**: REST responses match OpenAPI spec
- **Validation**: Response schema, status codes, headers

### Load Tests (Key endpoints)
- **Framework**: k6
- **Location**: `tests/load/`
- **Scope**: WebSocket connections, API throughput, database operations
- **Thresholds**: P95 < 100ms for reads, P95 < 200ms for writes

## Test Utilities Package

```typescript
// packages/test-utils/src/index.ts

// Database utilities
export { createTestDatabase, seedTestData, cleanupTestData } from './db';

// API testing
export { createTestClient, mockAuthContext, assertApiResponse } from './api';

// WebSocket testing
export { createTestWsClient, waitForWsEvent, mockWsHub } from './ws';

// Agent testing
export { mockAgentDriver, createTestAgent, simulateAgentOutput } from './agent';

// Time utilities
export { freezeTime, advanceTime, mockDateNow } from './time';

// Assertion helpers
export { expectApiError, expectEvent, expectDatabaseState } from './assertions';

// Logging capture
export { captureTestLogs, assertLogContains, getTestLogSummary } from './logging';
```

## Test Logging Standards

All tests must produce **detailed, structured logs** for debugging failures:

```typescript
// Pattern for test setup logging
test.beforeEach(({ testInfo }) => {
  testLogger.info('Test starting', {
    testName: testInfo.title,
    file: testInfo.file,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  });
});

// Pattern for assertion context
expect.extend({
  toHaveAgentStatus(agent, status) {
    const pass = agent.status === status;
    testLogger.debug('Assertion: toHaveAgentStatus', {
      agentId: agent.id,
      expected: status,
      actual: agent.status,
      pass,
    });
    return { pass, message: () => `Expected ${agent.id} to have status ${status}, got ${agent.status}` };
  },
});

// Pattern for async operation logging
async function waitForCondition(check: () => boolean, timeout: number) {
  const start = Date.now();
  while (!check() && Date.now() - start < timeout) {
    testLogger.trace('Waiting for condition', { elapsed: Date.now() - start });
    await sleep(50);
  }
  testLogger.debug('Wait completed', { 
    elapsed: Date.now() - start, 
    success: check(),
  });
}
```

## File Locations

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `packages/test-utils/` | Shared test utilities |
| `apps/gateway/src/**/*.test.ts` | Unit tests (co-located) |
| `apps/gateway/tests/integration/` | Integration tests |
| `apps/web/src/**/*.test.tsx` | Component unit tests |
| `tests/e2e/` | Playwright E2E tests |
| `tests/contract/` | OpenAPI contract tests |
| `tests/load/` | k6 load tests |
| `tests/fixtures/` | Shared test data |

## CI Pipeline Integration

```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
name: Test Suite

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  unit:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install
      - run: bun test --coverage
      - run: bun run test:coverage-check  # Fail if below threshold

  integration:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:  # For certain integration tests
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test
    steps:
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install
      - run: bun test:integration

  e2e:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install
      - run: bunx playwright install --with-deps
      - run: bun run build
      - run: bun test:e2e
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: failure()
        with:
          name: playwright-traces
          path: tests/e2e/test-results/

  contract:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install
      - run: bun test:contract

  load:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: grafana/k6-action@v0.3.1
        with:
          filename: tests/load/api.k6.js
```

## Testing Requirements for Each Bead

Every feature bead MUST include:

1. **Unit Test Cases** (specific scenarios to test)
2. **Integration Test Cases** (API/service combinations)
3. **E2E Test Cases** (user journeys, if UI involved)
4. **Performance Test Cases** (if latency-sensitive)
5. **Failure Mode Tests** (what happens when dependencies fail)

Example testing section format for feature beads:

```markdown
## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Test A: Description of what's being tested
- [ ] Test B: Description
- [ ] Test C: Edge case for X

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Test D: API endpoint with real database
- [ ] Test E: Service combination scenario

### E2E Tests (if applicable)
- [ ] Test F: User journey description

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Test G: Behavior when dependency X is unavailable
- [ ] Test H: Behavior on timeout

### Performance Tests
- [ ] P95 latency < Xms for operation Y
```


## Acceptance Criteria

### Test Infrastructure
- [ ] packages/test-utils package created with all utilities
- [ ] Database utilities support SQLite and PostgreSQL
- [ ] WebSocket test client supports reconnection testing
- [ ] Agent mock driver is feature-complete
- [ ] Time utilities allow deterministic time-based tests

### Test Runner Configuration
- [ ] Bun test configured with coverage thresholds
- [ ] Playwright configured with all browsers
- [ ] k6 configured with CI-appropriate thresholds
- [ ] Test reports generated in CI

### CI/CD Integration
- [ ] Unit tests run on every push
- [ ] Integration tests run on every push
- [ ] E2E tests run on PR
- [ ] Load tests run on PR with baselines
- [ ] Coverage reports posted to PR

### Logging Standards
- [ ] Test logger configured and documented
- [ ] Failure logs include full context
- [ ] Test artifacts preserved on failure
- [ ] Log verbosity configurable via environment

## Reference
- PLAN.md §25 (Testing Strategy)
- AGENTS.md (Testing conventions)

-{"id":"flywheel_gateway-dje","title":"FEAT: Developer Utilities Auto-Install (giil, csctf)","description":"## Background

Flywheel Gateway provides first-class integration with **developer utilities** that improve AI-agent workflows, with a strong bias for:

- **Zero-setup** (detect + install guidance)
- **Deterministic automation** (machine-readable output)
- **Safe-by-default** (no credential leaks; no path traversal)

This bead covers two utilities defined in `docs/PLAN.md` §17.7 and the repo’s AGENTS.md:

1. **giil** — download cloud-hosted images (iCloud/Dropbox/Google Photos/Drive, etc.) for AI visual analysis.
2. **csctf** — convert AI chat share links into clean Markdown/HTML transcripts for archiving/search.

These are optional utilities. When missing, the system must degrade gracefully with actionable guidance.

## Utility Management (PLAN.md §17.7.1)

### Data Model

```ts
// packages/shared/src/types/utilities.ts

export interface DeveloperUtility {
  name: string;           // "giil" | "csctf"
  description: string;
  version: string;        // desired/known-good
  installCommand: string; // one-liner install
  checkCommand: string;   // verify installation
  installed: boolean;
  installedVersion?: string;
  lastCheckedAt?: Date;
}
```

### Known Utilities

```ts
const UTILITIES: DeveloperUtility[] = [
  {
    name: 'giil',
    description: 'Download cloud photos for AI visual analysis',
    version: '3.1.0',
    installCommand: 'curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/giil/main/install.sh | bash',
    checkCommand: 'giil --version',
  },
  {
    name: 'csctf',
    description: 'Convert AI chat share links to Markdown/HTML',
    version: '0.4.5',
    installCommand: 'curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/chat_shared_conversation_to_file/main/install.sh | bash',
    checkCommand: 'csctf --version',
  },
];
```

## REST API Endpoints (PLAN.md §17.7.2)

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/utilities` | List all utilities with install status |
| `POST` | `/utilities/{name}/install` | Install a utility |
| `POST` | `/utilities/{name}/update` | Update a utility |
| `GET` | `/utilities/doctor` | Check all utilities health |

## giil Integration (PLAN.md §17.7.3)

giil enables agents (and humans) to pull screenshots/images from cloud share links into a local artifact that can be attached to agent context or used for debugging UI.

```ts
interface GiilRequest {
  url: string; // iCloud/Dropbox/Google share URL
  outputDir?: string;
  format?: 'file' | 'json' | 'base64';
}

interface GiilResponse {
  success: boolean;
  path?: string;
  width?: number;
  height?: number;
  captureMethod?: 'download' | 'cdn' | 'element' | 'viewport';
  error?: string;
}
```

## csctf Integration (PLAN.md §17.7.4)

csctf enables archiving AI conversations for knowledge management, documentation, and downstream indexing into CASS.

```ts
interface CsctfRequest {
  url: string; // ChatGPT/Gemini/Grok/Claude share URL
  outputDir?: string;
  formats?: ('md' | 'html')[];
  publishToGhPages?: boolean;
}

interface CsctfResponse {
  success: boolean;
  markdownPath?: string;
  htmlPath?: string;
  title?: string;
  messageCount?: number;
  error?: string;
}
```

## UX Requirements

- Utilities page shows installed/missing status, version, and a single “Doctor” button.
- Missing utilities show copy-paste install command (and optional one-click install if configured).
- Running giil/csctf from the UI shows progress, captures stdout/stderr, and stores a small artifact summary.

## Safety / Security Requirements

- Never log full share URLs or OAuth tokens. Prefer logging `urlHost` + `urlHash`.
- Validate `outputDir` (must be within an allowlisted base dir; reject traversal).
- Rate-limit install/update and run endpoints.
- Treat all subprocess execution as untrusted: set timeouts, capture output with size limits.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/utilities` returns both utilities with deterministic install status and versions.
- [ ] `/utilities/doctor` checks both utilities and returns structured health results.
- [ ] Install/update endpoints run the defined install commands with bounded logs and clear failure reasons.
- [ ] giil/csctf integrations return structured responses and store artifacts safely.
- [ ] Missing utilities produce actionable, copy-paste guidance; UI does not crash.

## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Utility detection: PATH lookup + version parsing is deterministic
- [ ] OutputDir validation rejects traversal and disallowed roots
- [ ] Subprocess wrapper enforces timeouts and log-size bounds

### Integration Tests

- [ ] When utilities are present (mocked): run a no-op invocation and capture structured results

### Failure Mode Tests

### E2E Tests

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + utility + version + attemptId; never log share URLs or tokens

+{"id":"flywheel_gateway-dje","title":"FEAT: Developer Utilities Auto-Install (giil, csctf)","description":"## Background

Flywheel Gateway provides first-class integration with **developer utilities** that improve AI-agent workflows, with a strong bias for:

- **Zero-setup** (detect + install guidance)
- **Deterministic automation** (machine-readable output)
- **Safe-by-default** (no credential leaks; no path traversal)

This bead covers two utilities defined in `docs/PLAN.md` §17.7 and the repo’s AGENTS.md:

1. **giil** — download cloud-hosted images (iCloud/Dropbox/Google Photos/Drive, etc.) for AI visual analysis.
2. **csctf** — convert AI chat share links into clean Markdown/HTML transcripts for archiving/search.

These are optional utilities. When missing, the system must degrade gracefully with actionable guidance.

## Utility Management (PLAN.md §17.7.1)

### Data Model

```ts
// packages/shared/src/types/utilities.ts

export interface DeveloperUtility {
  name: string;           // "giil" | "csctf"
  description: string;
  version: string;        // desired/known-good
  installCommand: string; // one-liner install
  checkCommand: string;   // verify installation
  installed: boolean;
  installedVersion?: string;
  lastCheckedAt?: Date;
}
```

### Known Utilities

```ts
const UTILITIES: DeveloperUtility[] = [
  {
    name: 'giil',
    description: 'Download cloud photos for AI visual analysis',
    version: '3.1.0',
    installCommand: 'curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/giil/main/install.sh | bash',
    checkCommand: 'giil --version',
  },
  {
    name: 'csctf',
    description: 'Convert AI chat share links to Markdown/HTML',
    version: '0.4.5',
    installCommand: 'curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/chat_shared_conversation_to_file/main/install.sh | bash',
    checkCommand: 'csctf --version',
  },
];
```

## REST API Endpoints (PLAN.md §17.7.2)

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/utilities` | List all utilities with install status |
| `POST` | `/utilities/{name}/install` | Install a utility |
| `POST` | `/utilities/{name}/update` | Update a utility |
| `GET` | `/utilities/doctor` | Check all utilities health |

## giil Integration (PLAN.md §17.7.3)

giil enables agents (and humans) to pull screenshots/images from cloud share links into a local artifact that can be attached to agent context or used for debugging UI.

```ts
interface GiilRequest {
  url: string; // iCloud/Dropbox/Google share URL
  outputDir?: string;
  format?: 'file' | 'json' | 'base64';
}

interface GiilResponse {
  success: boolean;
  path?: string;
  width?: number;
  height?: number;
  captureMethod?: 'download' | 'cdn' | 'element' | 'viewport';
  error?: string;
}
```

## csctf Integration (PLAN.md §17.7.4)

csctf enables archiving AI conversations for knowledge management, documentation, and downstream indexing into CASS.

```ts
interface CsctfRequest {
  url: string; // ChatGPT/Gemini/Grok/Claude share URL
  outputDir?: string;
  formats?: ('md' | 'html')[];
  publishToGhPages?: boolean;
}

interface CsctfResponse {
  success: boolean;
  markdownPath?: string;
  htmlPath?: string;
  title?: string;
  messageCount?: number;
  error?: string;
}
```

## UX Requirements

- Utilities page shows installed/missing status, version, and a single “Doctor” button.
- Missing utilities show copy-paste install command (and optional one-click install if configured).
- Running giil/csctf from the UI shows progress, captures stdout/stderr, and stores a small artifact summary.

## Safety / Security Requirements

- Never log full share URLs or OAuth tokens. Prefer logging `urlHost` + `urlHash`.
- Validate `outputDir` (must be within an allowlisted base dir; reject traversal).
- Rate-limit install/update and run endpoints.
- Treat all subprocess execution as untrusted: set timeouts, capture output with size limits.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/utilities` returns both utilities with deterministic install status and versions.
- [ ] `/utilities/doctor` checks both utilities and returns structured health results.
- [ ] Install/update endpoints run the defined install commands with bounded logs and clear failure reasons.
- [ ] giil/csctf integrations return structured responses and store artifacts safely.
- [ ] Missing utilities produce actionable, copy-paste guidance; UI does not crash.

## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Utility detection: PATH lookup + version parsing is deterministic
- [ ] OutputDir validation rejects traversal and disallowed roots
- [ ] Subprocess wrapper enforces timeouts and log-size bounds

### Integration Tests
- [ ] When utilities are missing: endpoints report unavailable and UI shows actionable install guidance
- [ ] When utilities are present (mocked): run a no-op invocation and capture structured results

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Non-zero exit / timeout / malformed output → mapped error taxonomy and safe logs

### E2E Tests
- [ ] UI: user visits Utilities page, runs Doctor, and sees install guidance

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + utility + version + attemptId; never log share URLs or tokens

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-e73","title":"Tmux Agent Driver","description":"## Overview

The Tmux Agent Driver provides a fallback mechanism for terminal-based agent execution when native drivers are unavailable or unsuitable. It leverages tmux for PTY management and session isolation.

## Background & Reasoning

While native agent drivers (Claude Code SDK, API-based) offer superior integration, some scenarios require terminal-based execution:

- Legacy agent tools without API support
- Debugging and development workflows
- Environments where native drivers fail
- Manual intervention capabilities

Tmux provides robust session management with:
- Detached execution (survives driver disconnect)
- Full PTY support (handles interactive prompts)
- Session capture (complete output history)
- Scriptable control interface

## Technical Architecture

### AgentDriver Interface Compliance

The Tmux driver implements the standard AgentDriver interface:

```typescript
interface AgentDriver {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  type: 'native' | 'api' | 'terminal';
  
  initialize(config: DriverConfig): Promise<void>;
  start(agent: Agent, task: Task): Promise<AgentSession>;
  stop(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;
  sendInput(sessionId: string, input: string): Promise<void>;
  getOutput(sessionId: string): Promise<OutputChunk[]>;
  getStatus(sessionId: string): Promise<SessionStatus>;
  cleanup(): Promise<void>;
}
```

### TmuxAgentDriver Implementation

```typescript
class TmuxAgentDriver implements AgentDriver {
  readonly id = 'tmux-driver';
  readonly name = 'Tmux Terminal Driver';
  readonly type = 'terminal';
  
  private sessions: Map<string, TmuxSession> = new Map();
  private tmuxSocketPath: string;
  
  async initialize(config: TmuxDriverConfig): Promise<void> {
    // Verify tmux installation
    // Create dedicated socket for isolation
    // Set up output capture directory
  }
  
  async start(agent: Agent, task: Task): Promise<AgentSession> {
    // Create new tmux session with unique name
    // Configure PTY dimensions
    // Start agent command
    // Begin output capture
    // Return session handle
  }
  
  async stop(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
    // Send SIGTERM to session
    // Wait for graceful shutdown
    // Force kill if timeout
    // Clean up session resources
  }
  
  async sendInput(sessionId: string, input: string): Promise<void> {
    // Send keys to tmux session
    // Handle special characters
    // Support escape sequences
  }
  
  async getOutput(sessionId: string): Promise<OutputChunk[]> {
    // Capture pane contents
    // Parse into structured chunks
    // Track output position
    // Handle ANSI codes
  }
  
  async getStatus(sessionId: string): Promise<SessionStatus> {
    // Check if session exists
    // Get running process info
    // Calculate resource usage
    // Return structured status
  }
  
  async cleanup(): Promise<void> {
    // Kill all managed sessions
    // Remove socket file
    // Clean up capture directory
  }
}
```

### PTY Management

```typescript
interface TmuxSession {
  sessionName: string;
  windowId: string;
  paneId: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  lastActivity: Date;
  outputFile: string;
  outputPosition: number;
  pid?: number;
}

interface TmuxManager {
  // Session lifecycle
  createSession(name: string, command: string): Promise<TmuxSession>;
  destroySession(name: string): Promise<void>;
  listSessions(): Promise<TmuxSession[]>;
  
  // Input/Output
  sendKeys(session: string, keys: string): Promise<void>;
  capturePane(session: string, options: CaptureOptions): Promise<string>;
  
  // Session management
  hasSession(name: string): Promise<boolean>;
  getSessionPid(name: string): Promise<number | null>;
  resizePane(session: string, width: number, height: number): Promise<void>;
}
```

### Output Capture Strategy

Two complementary capture mechanisms:

1. **Pipe Capture** (for real-time streaming)
   ```bash
   tmux pipe-pane -t $SESSION -o "cat >> /path/to/output.log"
   ```

2. **Pane Capture** (for complete history)
   ```bash
   tmux capture-pane -t $SESSION -p -S - -E -
   ```

```typescript
interface OutputCapture {
  // Real-time streaming via pipe
  startCapture(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;
  stopCapture(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;
  
  // On-demand full capture
  captureAll(sessionId: string): Promise<string>;
  
  // Incremental capture since last read
  captureNew(sessionId: string): Promise<OutputChunk[]>;
  
  // ANSI processing
  parseAnsi(raw: string): ParsedOutput;
  stripAnsi(raw: string): string;
}

interface OutputChunk {
  timestamp: Date;
  content: string;
  type: 'stdout' | 'stderr' | 'prompt';
  ansiCodes?: AnsiCode[];
}
```

### Error Handling

```typescript
class TmuxDriverError extends Error {
  constructor(
    message: string,
    public code: TmuxErrorCode,
    public sessionId?: string,
    public cause?: Error
  ) {
    super(message);
  }
}

enum TmuxErrorCode {
  TMUX_NOT_FOUND = 'TMUX_NOT_FOUND',
  SESSION_EXISTS = 'SESSION_EXISTS',
  SESSION_NOT_FOUND = 'SESSION_NOT_FOUND',
  COMMAND_FAILED = 'COMMAND_FAILED',
  CAPTURE_FAILED = 'CAPTURE_FAILED',
  SEND_KEYS_FAILED = 'SEND_KEYS_FAILED',
  SOCKET_ERROR = 'SOCKET_ERROR',
}
```

## File Locations

- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/TmuxAgentDriver.ts` - Main driver implementation
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/TmuxManager.ts` - Low-level tmux operations
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/OutputCapture.ts` - Output capture logic
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/AnsiParser.ts` - ANSI code processing
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/errors.ts` - Error definitions
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/tmux/__tests__/` - Test files
- `packages/agent-drivers/src/index.ts` - Export driver

## Configuration

```typescript
interface TmuxDriverConfig {
  // Tmux executable path (default: 'tmux')
  tmuxPath?: string;
  
  // Socket path for session isolation
  socketPath?: string;
  
  // Directory for output capture files
  captureDir?: string;
  
  // Default PTY dimensions
  defaultWidth?: number;   // default: 120
  defaultHeight?: number;  // default: 40
  
  // Session naming prefix
  sessionPrefix?: string;  // default: 'flywheel-'
  
  // Cleanup stale sessions on startup
  cleanupOnStart?: boolean;
  
  // Maximum concurrent sessions
  maxSessions?: number;
  
  // Output capture settings
  captureMode?: 'pipe' | 'pane' | 'both';
  captureInterval?: number;  // ms between captures
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Driver implements complete AgentDriver interface
- [ ] Sessions create successfully with unique names
- [ ] Input sent to sessions via sendKeys
- [ ] Output captured in real-time with <100ms latency
- [ ] Sessions survive driver restart (detached mode)
- [ ] ANSI escape codes properly parsed and handled
- [ ] Graceful shutdown sends SIGTERM before SIGKILL
- [ ] Resource cleanup removes all session artifacts
- [ ] Error messages provide actionable information
- [ ] Driver degrades gracefully when tmux unavailable

## Testing Requirements

- Unit tests for tmux command generation
- Integration tests with actual tmux sessions
- E2E tests for complete agent workflow
- Stress tests for concurrent session management
- Compatibility tests across tmux versions (2.x, 3.x)
- Cleanup tests for orphan session handling

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Driver session naming + attach target selection logic
- [ ] Exit parsing and state transition mapping

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Spawn tmux session in test env (stubbed) and capture output
- [ ] Attach/detach flow updates agent state and emits events

### E2E Tests
- [ ] UI: start tmux-backed agent, attach terminal, observe output
- [ ] UI: detach/terminate and confirm cleanup visible in status

### Logging
- [ ] Driver tests log `sessionName`, attach/detach actions, and exit codes; include correlation IDs
- [ ] UI artifacts capture terminal screenshot/trace when attach fails


## Dependencies

- tmux >= 2.6 (recommended 3.x)
- Node.js child_process for command execution
- fs/promises for file operations
- Optional: node-pty for enhanced PTY handling

## Security Considerations

- Session names sanitized to prevent injection
- Socket permissions restrict access
- Output files created with secure permissions
- Environment variables filtered before pass-through
- Command execution uses shell: false where possible

## Performance Notes

- Session creation: ~50ms
- Send keys latency: <10ms
- Output capture: ~20ms per capture
- Memory per session: ~1MB baseline
- Disk per session: varies by output volume

## References

- PLAN.md §5 - Agent Driver Architecture
- PLAN.md §6 - Terminal Execution Patterns
- tmux man page and documentation
- PTY best practices for terminal emulation","notes":"## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] TmuxManager command generation produces correct tmux syntax for all operations
- [ ] Session name sanitization prevents shell injection
- [ ] ANSI parser correctly extracts codes and strips for plain text
- [ ] Output chunk parsing preserves ordering and handles partial lines

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Session lifecycle: create → send input → capture output → terminate
- [ ] Session survives driver restart (detached mode verification)
- [ ] Concurrent sessions (up to maxSessions) operate independently
- [ ] PTY resize propagates correctly to running session

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Complete agent workflow: start task → stream output → complete
- [ ] Agent interrupt handling via SIGINT/SIGTERM

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Tmux not installed → clear error with install hint
- [ ] Session already exists → appropriate error code
- [ ] Orphan session cleanup on startup works correctly
- [ ] Socket permission errors → actionable error message

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Session creation < 100ms
- [ ] Output capture latency < 100ms
- [ ] Memory per session < 2MB baseline

### Compatibility Tests
- [ ] Works with tmux 2.6, 3.0, 3.3 (major versions)

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + sessionId + tmuxCommand + exitCode; never log raw output content","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T13:49:45.836617442-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:50:03.678879088-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-e73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-w55","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:02:05.926207108-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-ew1","title":"Auto-Healing Context Window Management","description":"## Background

Auto-Healing Context Window Management is a proactive system that monitors context window health and takes corrective action before problems occur. Rather than waiting for context overflow errors, this system maintains optimal context health through graduated interventions.

### Why This Matters

1. **Proactive vs Reactive**: Waiting for context overflow means lost work and degraded agent performance. Proactive management prevents these issues entirely.

2. **Seamless Experience**: Users and agents should never experience sudden context loss. Graduated interventions maintain continuity.

3. **Cost Optimization**: Compaction and summarization reduce token usage while preserving essential information.

4. **Agent Continuity**: When rotation is necessary, automatic context transfer ensures the new agent starts with full awareness.

## Technical Design

### Health Thresholds

```typescript
interface ContextHealthThresholds {
  // Warning level - start preparing
  warning: {
    percentage: 75;              // 75% of context used
    actions: ['log', 'event', 'prepare_summary'];
  };
  
  // Critical level - active intervention
  critical: {
    percentage: 85;              // 85% of context used
    actions: ['summarize', 'compact', 'event'];
  };
  
  // Emergency level - rotation required
  emergency: {
    percentage: 95;              // 95% of context used
    actions: ['checkpoint', 'rotate', 'transfer', 'event'];
  };
}

enum ContextHealthStatus {
  HEALTHY = 'healthy',           // < 75%
  WARNING = 'warning',           // 75-84%
  CRITICAL = 'critical',         // 85-94%
  EMERGENCY = 'emergency'        // >= 95%
}
```

### Health Monitor

```typescript
interface ContextHealth {
  sessionId: string;
  status: ContextHealthStatus;
  currentTokens: number;
  maxTokens: number;
  percentUsed: number;
  
  // Projections
  projectedOverflowInMessages: number | null;
  estimatedTimeToWarning: number | null;  // milliseconds
  
  // History
  tokenHistory: TokenHistoryEntry[];
  lastCompaction: Date | null;
  lastRotation: Date | null;
  
  // Recommendations
  recommendations: HealthRecommendation[];
}

interface TokenHistoryEntry {
  timestamp: Date;
  tokens: number;
  delta: number;
  event: string;  // 'message', 'compaction', 'rotation', etc.
}

interface HealthRecommendation {
  action: 'summarize' | 'compact' | 'rotate' | 'none';
  urgency: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'critical';
  reason: string;
  estimatedTokenSavings: number;
}

class ContextHealthMonitor {
  private healthCache = new Map<string, ContextHealth>();
  private checkInterval = 10000; // 10 seconds
  
  async checkHealth(sessionId: string): Promise<ContextHealth> {
    const session = await this.sessionService.get(sessionId);
    const currentTokens = await this.tokenizer.countSession(session);
    const maxTokens = this.getModelLimit(session.model);
    const percentUsed = (currentTokens / maxTokens) * 100;
    
    const status = this.determineStatus(percentUsed);
    const history = await this.getTokenHistory(sessionId);
    
    const health: ContextHealth = {
      sessionId,
      status,
      currentTokens,
      maxTokens,
      percentUsed,
      projectedOverflowInMessages: this.projectOverflow(history, maxTokens),
      estimatedTimeToWarning: this.estimateTimeToThreshold(history, 75, maxTokens),
      tokenHistory: history,
      lastCompaction: await this.getLastCompaction(sessionId),
      lastRotation: await this.getLastRotation(sessionId),
      recommendations: this.generateRecommendations(status, percentUsed, history)
    };
    
    this.healthCache.set(sessionId, health);
    
    // Trigger actions based on status
    await this.handleStatus(health);
    
    return health;
  }
  
  private determineStatus(percentUsed: number): ContextHealthStatus {
    if (percentUsed >= 95) return ContextHealthStatus.EMERGENCY;
    if (percentUsed >= 85) return ContextHealthStatus.CRITICAL;
    if (percentUsed >= 75) return ContextHealthStatus.WARNING;
    return ContextHealthStatus.HEALTHY;
  }
  
  private async handleStatus(health: ContextHealth): Promise<void> {
    switch (health.status) {
      case ContextHealthStatus.WARNING:
        await this.handleWarning(health);
        break;
      case ContextHealthStatus.CRITICAL:
        await this.handleCritical(health);
        break;
      case ContextHealthStatus.EMERGENCY:
        await this.handleEmergency(health);
        break;
    }
  }
}
```

### Proactive Summarization

```typescript
interface SummarizationConfig {
  // Target reduction
  targetReduction: number;       // Target token reduction (e.g., 0.3 = 30%)
  
  // What to summarize
  summarizable: {
    conversationHistory: boolean;
    searchResults: boolean;
    beadContent: boolean;
  };
  
  // Preservation rules
  preserve: {
    lastNMessages: number;       // Always keep last N messages verbatim
    recentMinutes: number;       // Keep messages from last N minutes
    keyDecisions: boolean;       // Preserve decision points
    errorContext: boolean;       // Preserve error-related context
  };
}

class ProactiveSummarizer {
  async summarize(
    sessionId: string,
    config: SummarizationConfig
  ): Promise<SummarizationResult> {
    const session = await this.sessionService.get(sessionId);
    const beforeTokens = await this.tokenizer.countSession(session);
    
    // Identify summarizable content
    const summarizable = this.identifySummarizable(session, config);
    
    // Generate summaries
    const summaries = await Promise.all(
      summarizable.map(content => this.generateSummary(content))
    );
    
    // Replace content with summaries
    const updatedSession = this.applySummaries(session, summaries, config.preserve);
    
    const afterTokens = await this.tokenizer.countSession(updatedSession);
    
    return {
      beforeTokens,
      afterTokens,
      reduction: beforeTokens - afterTokens,
      reductionPercent: ((beforeTokens - afterTokens) / beforeTokens) * 100,
      summarizedSections: summaries.map(s => s.section),
      preservedSections: this.getPreservedSections(config.preserve)
    };
  }
  
  private async generateSummary(content: SummarizableContent): Promise<Summary> {
    // Use LLM to generate concise summary
    const prompt = this.buildSummaryPrompt(content);
    const summary = await this.llm.complete(prompt);
    
    return {
      section: content.section,
      originalTokens: content.tokens,
      summaryTokens: await this.tokenizer.count(summary),
      summary,
      keyPoints: this.extractKeyPoints(summary)
    };
  }
}
```

### Agent Rotation with Context Transfer

```typescript
interface RotationConfig {
  // When to rotate
  triggers: {
    contextPercentage: number;   // Rotate at this % (default: 95)
    messageCount: number;        // Rotate after N messages
    timeMinutes: number;         // Rotate after N minutes
  };
  
  // How to transfer
  transfer: {
    includeFullSummary: boolean;
    includeRecentMessages: number;
    includeActiveBeads: boolean;
    includeMemoryRules: boolean;
  };
  
  // New agent setup
  newAgent: {
    model: string;
    warmupPrompt: string;
  };
}

interface ContextTransfer {
  sourceSessionId: string;
  targetSessionId: string;
  checkpointId: string;
  
  // Transferred content
  summary: string;
  recentMessages: Message[];
  activeBeads: string[];
  memoryRules: string[];
  
  // Metadata
  sourceTokens: number;
  transferTokens: number;
  compressionRatio: number;
}

class AgentRotationManager {
  async rotate(
    sessionId: string,
    config: RotationConfig
  ): Promise<RotationResult> {
    // 1. Create checkpoint before rotation
    const checkpoint = await this.checkpointService.create(sessionId, {
      trigger: 'rotation',
      metadata: { rotationConfig: config }
    });
    
    // 2. Build context transfer
    const transfer = await this.buildTransfer(sessionId, config.transfer);
    
    // 3. Create new session
    const newSession = await this.sessionService.create({
      model: config.newAgent.model,
      parentSessionId: sessionId,
      rotatedFrom: checkpoint.id
    });
    
    // 4. Initialize new agent with transferred context
    await this.initializeRotatedAgent(newSession.id, transfer, config.newAgent);
    
    // 5. Mark old session as rotated
    await this.sessionService.update(sessionId, {
      status: 'rotated',
      rotatedTo: newSession.id
    });
    
    // 6. Emit rotation event
    await this.events.emit('context.emergency_rotated', {
      sourceSessionId: sessionId,
      targetSessionId: newSession.id,
      checkpointId: checkpoint.id,
      transfer: {
        sourceTokens: transfer.sourceTokens,
        transferTokens: transfer.transferTokens,
        compressionRatio: transfer.compressionRatio
      }
    });
    
    return {
      newSessionId: newSession.id,
      checkpointId: checkpoint.id,
      transfer
    };
  }
  
  private async buildTransfer(
    sessionId: string,
    config: RotationConfig['transfer']
  ): Promise<ContextTransfer> {
    const session = await this.sessionService.get(sessionId);
    
    // Generate comprehensive summary
    const summary = config.includeFullSummary
      ? await this.summarizer.generateFullSummary(session)
      : await this.summarizer.generateBriefSummary(session);
    
    // Get recent messages
    const recentMessages = session.messages.slice(-config.includeRecentMessages);
    
    // Get active beads
    const activeBeads = config.includeActiveBeads
      ? await this.beadService.getActiveBeads(sessionId)
      : [];
    
    // Get relevant memory rules
    const memoryRules = config.includeMemoryRules
      ? await this.memoryService.getSessionRules(sessionId)
      : [];
    
    const sourceTokens = await this.tokenizer.countSession(session);
    const transferTokens = await this.tokenizer.count(
      this.formatTransfer(summary, recentMessages, activeBeads, memoryRules)
    );
    
    return {
      sourceSessionId: sessionId,
      targetSessionId: '', // Will be set after new session creation
      checkpointId: '', // Will be set after checkpoint creation
      summary,
      recentMessages,
      activeBeads: activeBeads.map(b => b.id),
      memoryRules: memoryRules.map(r => r.content),
      sourceTokens,
      transferTokens,
      compressionRatio: sourceTokens / transferTokens
    };
  }
}
```

### WebSocket Events

```typescript
// Context warning event
{
  event: 'context.warning',
  data: {
    sessionId: string,
    percentUsed: number,
    currentTokens: number,
    maxTokens: number,
    recommendations: HealthRecommendation[]
  }
}

// Context compacted event
{
  event: 'context.compacted',
  data: {
    sessionId: string,
    beforeTokens: number,
    afterTokens: number,
    reduction: number,
    reductionPercent: number,
    method: 'summarization' | 'pruning' | 'both'
  }
}

// Emergency rotation event
{
  event: 'context.emergency_rotated',
  data: {
    sourceSessionId: string,
    targetSessionId: string,
    checkpointId: string,
    reason: 'context_overflow' | 'manual' | 'scheduled',
    transfer: {
      sourceTokens: number,
      transferTokens: number,
      compressionRatio: number
    }
  }
}
```

## API Design

### REST Endpoints

```typescript
// Get context health
GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/context/health
Response: ContextHealth

// Trigger manual compaction
POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/context/compact
Request: { strategy?: 'summarize' | 'prune' | 'both' }
Response: CompactionResult

// Trigger manual rotation
POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/context/rotate
Request: { config?: RotationConfig }
Response: RotationResult

// Get context history
GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/context/history
Query: { since?: Date, limit?: number }
Response: { entries: TokenHistoryEntry[] }
```

## Configuration

```typescript
interface ContextHealthConfig {
  // Thresholds
  thresholds: ContextHealthThresholds;
  
  // Monitoring
  monitoring: {
    checkIntervalMs: number;     // How often to check health
    historyRetentionHours: number;
  };
  
  // Auto-healing
  autoHealing: {
    enabled: boolean;
    summarizationEnabled: boolean;
    rotationEnabled: boolean;
  };
  
  // Summarization
  summarization: SummarizationConfig;
  
  // Rotation
  rotation: RotationConfig;
}
```

## File Locations

- **Primary Service**: `apps/gateway/src/services/context-health.service.ts`
- **Health Monitor**: `apps/gateway/src/services/context-health-monitor.service.ts`
- **Summarizer**: `apps/gateway/src/services/context-summarizer.service.ts`
- **Rotation Manager**: `apps/gateway/src/services/agent-rotation.service.ts`
- **Types**: `apps/gateway/src/types/context-health.types.ts`
- **Controller**: `apps/gateway/src/controllers/context-health.controller.ts`
- **Tests**: `apps/gateway/src/services/__tests__/context-health.service.test.ts`

## Dependencies

- Context service (for current context state)
- Checkpoint service (for pre-rotation checkpoints)
- Session service (for session management)
- Tokenizer service (for token counting)
- LLM service (for summarization)
- WebSocket gateway (for events)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Health Monitoring**
   - [ ] Accurately calculates context utilization percentage
   - [ ] Correctly determines health status based on thresholds
   - [ ] Projects future overflow based on token history
   - [ ] Generates actionable recommendations

2. **Warning Level (75%)**
   - [ ] Emits context.warning event
   - [ ] Logs warning with recommendations
   - [ ] Prepares summarization strategy

3. **Critical Level (85%)**
   - [ ] Automatically triggers summarization
   - [ ] Achieves >20% token reduction
   - [ ] Preserves recent messages and key decisions
   - [ ] Emits context.compacted event

4. **Emergency Level (95%)**
   - [ ] Creates checkpoint before rotation
   - [ ] Builds comprehensive context transfer
   - [ ] Creates new session with transferred context
   - [ ] Emits context.emergency_rotated event
   - [ ] New agent starts with full awareness

5. **Context Transfer**
   - [ ] Includes summary of full session history
   - [ ] Includes recent messages verbatim
   - [ ] Includes active bead references
   - [ ] Includes relevant memory rules
   - [ ] Achieves >5:1 compression ratio

6. **Observability**
   - [ ] All events include relevant metrics
   - [ ] Token history is queryable
   - [ ] Health status is exposed via API
   - [ ] Prometheus metrics for health status

## Reference

- PLAN.md Section 7.6 - Auto-Healing Context Window Management


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Threshold evaluation chooses the correct action (warn → compact → rotate) based on usage percent
- [ ] Agent capability detection selects correct compaction commands per driver/agent type
- [ ] Rotation decision logic avoids thrash (cooldowns) and preserves required context

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Simulated high-usage session triggers compaction workflow and emits expected events

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Compaction attempt fails → fallback behavior is safe and surfaces actionable guidance

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Covered by `flywheel_gateway-tz4` critical-path E2E suite; add a focused E2E spec if this feature introduces unique user workflows
- [ ] Failure mode E2E validates actionable recovery UI/messages (with artifacts captured)

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + agentId + usageBefore/After + method + reclaimedTokens

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-f8f","title":"EPIC: Phase 3 - Flywheel Integration","description":"## Overview
Phase 3 integrates with the full Flywheel ecosystem including Beads/BV, CASS, CM, UBS, and enables intelligent conflict resolution with AI assistance.

## Phase 3 Goal
Full integration with Flywheel ecosystem

## Key Deliverables

### Flywheel Tool Integration
- Beads/BV integration
  - BVClient for CRUD operations
  - Triage API (ready beads, blocked beads, triageWork)
  - Insights API (bottlenecks, keystones)
  - Galaxy View dependency visualization
  - Graph rendering with WebGL (react-force-graph)
- CASS search integration
  - Search across agent conversations
  - Session context retrieval
  - History indexing
- CM memory integration
  - Playbook rules retrieval
  - Context queries before tasks
  - Learning feedback loop
- UBS scanner integration
  - Scan before commit
  - Auto-bead creation for findings
  - Fix workflow integration

### Advanced Conflict Resolution
- Intelligent Conflict Resolution Assistant
  - AI-powered merge suggestions
  - Semantic understanding of changes
  - Auto-resolution rules
  - User preference learning

### Session Management
- First-Class Session Handoff Protocol
  - Structured context transfer
  - Resource handover
  - Pending work queue transfer
  - Handoff audit trail

### Collaboration Visualization
- Real-Time Agent Collaboration Graph
  - Live visualization of agent activity
  - File heat maps
  - Communication flow visualization
  - Interaction timeline

### Safety & Coordination
- SLB Safety Guardrails integration
  - Pre-flight checks
  - Approval workflows
  - Escalation paths
- Git coordination service
  - Branch management
  - Merge orchestration
  - Conflict prevention

### Fleet Management
- RU (Repo Updater) integration
  - Fleet status dashboard
  - Multi-repo sync status
  - Agent-sweep orchestration
  - Three-phase review workflow

### DCG Advanced Features
- Allowlist management UI
- False positive feedback loop
- Pack configuration UI
- Custom pattern editor

## Phase Completion Criteria
- [ ] BV triage returns ready work for agents
- [ ] Galaxy View renders dependency graph interactively
- [ ] CASS search finds relevant prior solutions
- [ ] CM provides context rules for tasks
- [ ] UBS findings create beads automatically
- [ ] Conflict Resolution Assistant suggests merges
- [ ] Session handoffs preserve full context
- [ ] Collaboration graph shows live agent activity
- [ ] RU fleet status visible in dashboard
- [ ] Agent-sweep runs through Gateway

## Testing Requirements
- Unit test coverage >80% for all clients
- Integration tests for all Flywheel tool APIs
- E2E tests for triage → work → complete cycle
- Visual regression tests for Galaxy View
- Performance tests for graph rendering (1000+ nodes)

### Logging
- [ ] Unit/integration tests emit structured logs with `correlationId` + relevant entity IDs (never secrets)
- [ ] E2E tests capture artifacts (trace/video/screenshot/console logs) and surface failure context per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`


## Success Criteria

- [ ] Full flywheel loop works: Beads/BV → Agent Mail coordination → Gateway execution → UBS scan → CASS index/search → CM rule extraction → context packs
- [ ] Intelligent conflict resolution produces actionable suggestions and logs rationale/audit trail
- [ ] First-class session handoff protocol transfers context + resources safely (with acknowledgements)
- [ ] Git coordination + RU orchestration can run end-to-end on a repo fleet (dry-run + execute modes)
- [ ] Tests: contract tests cover all integrated REST surfaces + E2E covers critical user workflows per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

## Reference

- `flywheel_gateway-y19` (embedded `docs/PLAN.md` spec snapshot)
